direct_competitors.name: Nourish

pricing.range: $0 (insurance-covered) for patients; platform takes fee from insurance reimbursements
pricing.detail: Insurance-based model. Patients pay $0-copay. Nourish bills insurance directly. Dietitians are 1099 contractors.

strengths: Largest RD network in US (3000+ dietitians)
AI-powered platform with meal logging and symptom tracking
Strong brand recognition and media coverage (Forbes, TechCrunch)
weaknesses: Insurance-dependent model not portable to markets without coverage
BBB complaints about billing and cancellation
Provider complaints about platform control over patient relationships

direct_competitors.name: Fay Nutrition

pricing.detail: Insurance-based model similar to Nourish. $50M Series B at $500M valuation.

strengths: Large RD network across all 50 US states
Partnership with food service delivery platforms
weaknesses: 182 Trustpilot reviews suggest smaller scale than Nourish

traction.funding: $75M total ($25M emerged from stealth + $50M Series B)

direct_competitors.name: Berry Street

pricing.detail: Insurance-based marketplace. 95% sessions insurance-covered. RDs are 1099 contractors.

weaknesses: Reddit complaints about excessive cut from insurance payments
direct_competitors.estimated_size: Late Series A/early B, growing rapidly

direct_competitors.name: Practice Better

pricing.detail: Starter $25/mo (10 clients), Professional $69/mo (300 clients), Plus $99/mo (unlimited), Team $155/mo (2+ practitioners)

strengths: Acquired That Clean Life for meal planning integration
weaknesses: Not focused on LATAM or Spanish language
Premium features locked behind higher tiers

direct_competitors.name: Healthie

pricing.detail: Core $19/mo (10 clients), Essentials $49/mo (250 clients), Plus $129/mo (unlimited), Group $149+/mo

strengths: Strong telehealth and insurance billing features
weaknesses: Higher price for clinical features many independents don't need

direct_competitors.name: Nutrium
strengths: Strong in Europe and Latin America
Localized food databases for multiple regions
direct_competitors.estimated_size: Series A, Portugal-based, millions of patients worldwide

direct_competitors.name: Foodzilla
strengths: White-label mobile app and web portal

indirect_competitors.name: Doctoralia
indirect_competitors.approach: General healthcare marketplace that includes nutritionists among many specialties. Patients find and book appointments with any type of doctor.
indirect_competitors.relevance: Dominant healthcare marketplace in LATAM (Mexico, Colombia, Chile, Argentina). Already has nutritionists listed. Could be the default patient-discovery channel for nutritionists, but lacks nutrition-specific tools like meal planning or AI diet generation.

indirect_competitors.name: That Clean Life
indirect_competitors.approach: Recipe-focused meal planning software that generates beautiful PDF exports. Acquired by Practice Better.
indirect_competitors.relevance: Strong in recipe-based meal planning but no client management, no marketplace, no AI. Now part of Practice Better ecosystem.

indirect_competitors.name: SimplePractice
indirect_competitors.approach: General practice management for therapists and health professionals. Not nutrition-specific.
indirect_competitors.relevance: Some nutritionists use it for scheduling/billing but lack nutrition-specific features like meal planning or food databases.

adjacent_competitors.name: Noom
adjacent_competitors.current_focus: Consumer weight loss app using behavioral psychology and coaching. B2C model.
adjacent_competitors.pivot_threat: Could expand to B2B nutritionist tools or add professional-grade meal planning for RDs.
adjacent_competitors.evidence: Noom has pivoted multiple times (from fitness to weight loss to clinical weight management). Has GLP-1 companion programs.

adjacent_competitors.name: Zoe
adjacent_competitors.current_focus: Microbiome and personalized nutrition testing. Consumer subscription model. $118M total funding.
adjacent_competitors.pivot_threat: Could add professional dietitian tools or marketplace connecting consumers with RDs based on test results.
adjacent_competitors.evidence: Expanding from UK to US market. Raised $15M in 2024 for US expansion. Personalized nutrition data could feed into dietitian-patient matching.

failed_competitors.name: Babylon Health
failed_competitors.reason_failed: Telehealth company valued at $2B went bankrupt. Overexpanded, couldn't achieve unit economics at scale. General telehealth, not nutrition-specific, but relevant to the telehealth marketplace model.

failed_competitors.name: Forward Health
failed_competitors.reason_failed: AI-powered healthcare startup burned through $400M. Doctor-in-a-box model couldn't scale. Relevant lesson: hardware+software healthcare plays are capital intensive.

market_gaps.gap: LATAM/Spanish-language nutrition software with localized food databases
sources: Nutrium limited LATAM coverage despite Portuguese support

market_gaps.gap: AI-powered meal plan generation missing from most practice management tools
sources: Practice Better reviews noting basic meal planning
Healthie reviews noting basic nutrition tools

market_gaps.gap: Marketplace for patient discovery combined with practice management tools
sources: Berry Street Reddit complaints about RD exploitation

market_gaps.gap: High platform fees cutting into dietitian earnings in marketplace models
sources: Berry Street Reddit threads about excessive cut
Reddit r/dietetics discussions on marketplace economics

market_gaps.gap: Integrated follow-up and patient adherence tracking beyond basic food journals
sources: Comparison articles noting basic food journal limitations
